meta.title: Secure ModBus (TCP/802)
meta.description:
Daniel Clarke of Schneider Electric presents their proposal to add security to Modbus. The goals were to maintain simplicity, maintain deployed base, and allow for a mixed environment where some comms were secured and others were not based on criticality. The focus is on mutual authentication and encryption is optional.

6:30  The advantages of using TLS to secure industrial protocols

8:45  The Secure Modbus packet structure and basics

14:15  Extending the certificates to include role based authorization

16:20  PKI basics for Secure Modbus

Interesting point in the Q&A that this is for Modbus TCP only, not serial. One questioner from an electric utility said they don't want TCP because it increases their NERC CIP regulatory burden.
